---
title: "Swiss City Streets: a Gender Bias?"
output:
    ioslides_presentation:
        theme: flatly
        widescreen: true
css: city-streets.css
author: "Alexandre Masselot"
date: "2026-02-27"
---
```{r package_setup, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# https://chatgpt.com/c/699f73a9-eb58-8337-9f49-6b0525b21ff7
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

if (requireNamespace("rstudioapi", quietly = TRUE) && rstudioapi::isAvailable()) {
  # Interactive in RStudio: use the current editor document path
  rmd_path <- rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path
} else {
  # When knitting/rendering: knitr knows the input
  rmd_path <- knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)
}
if (!is.null(rmd_path) && nzchar(rmd_path)) {
  knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = dirname(normalizePath(rmd_path)))
  setwd(dirname(normalizePath(rmd_path)))
}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.width = 12,   # increase width
  fig.height = 6,
  out.width = "100%",   # VERY IMPORTANT
  fig.bg = "black",     # ensure the full device canvas is black
  dev.args = list(bg = "black") # for devices that honor bg via dev.args
)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(DBI)
library(RSQLite)
```
```{r loading_data}


# Path to the SQLite DB relative to this notebook
db_path <- file.path("..", "databases", "city_streets.db")
stopifnot(file.exists(db_path))

con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), dbname = db_path)

query <- "
SELECT
    st.name,
    st.is_people_name,
    st.gender_name,
    seg.street_name,
    seg.city,
    seg.id AS id_segment,
    p.lat,
    p.lon
FROM points AS p
JOIN segments AS seg
    ON p.id_segment = seg.id
JOIN streets AS st
    ON seg.street_name = st.name
"
# Connect, ensure disconnection at the end of the chunk
con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), dbname = db_path)




res <- dbSendQuery(con, query)
df.street.points <- dbFetch(res)
dbClearResult(res)
dbDisconnect(con)


gender_names <- c("FEMALE", "MALE", "NEUTRAL", "NOT_HUMAN")
gender_colors <- c(
  "FEMALE" = "#f4cae4",
  "MALE" = rgb(93, 173, 226, maxColorValue = 255),
  "NEUTRAL" = "#666666",
  "NOT_HUMAN" = "#666666"
)
```
```{r data_distribution}
gender_distribution <- df.street.points %>%
  distinct(city, gender_name, street_name, is_people_name) %>%
  mutate(gender_name = factor(gender_name, levels = gender_names)) %>%
  filter(gender_name != "NOT_HUMAN") %>%
  group_by(city, gender_name, is_people_name) %>%
  arrange(desc(gender_name)) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  mutate(
    city_total = sum(n, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct = n / city_total
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

is_people_distribution <- df.street.points %>%
  distinct(city, is_people_name, street_name) %>%
  group_by(city, is_people_name) %>%
  mutate(is_people_name = ifelse(is_people_name == 0, FALSE, TRUE)) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

nb_streets_per_city <- df.street.points %>%
  distinct(city, street_name) %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")
```

# Counting

## Steet count

```{r plot_count_streets}
black_bg_theme_barplot <- function() {
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    panel.grid = element_line(colour = "#333333"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "white"),
    plot.title = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold")
  )
}

nb_streets_per_city <- nb_streets_per_city %>% arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(nb_streets_per_city, aes(x = n, y = reorder(city, n))) +
  geom_col(fill = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "How Many Streets per City?",
    x = "",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  black_bg_theme_barplot()
```

## Streets with people names
```{r plot_count_people_nams}

ggplot(is_people_distribution, aes(x = n, y = reorder(city, n))) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = is_people_name)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c("TRUE" = "white", "FALSE" = "lightgrey"),
    breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE),
    labels = c("Yes", "No"),
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "How Many Streets with People Names?",
    x = "",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.98, 0.05),
    legend.justification = c("right", "bottom")
  ) +
  black_bg_theme_barplot()

```

# So, is there a gender bias?

## Streets Count by Gendered Names

```{r plot_count_by_gender}

ggplot(
  gender_distribution,
  aes(x = n, y = reorder(city, n), fill = gender_name)
) +
  geom_col(position = position_stack(reverse = TRUE)) +
  labs(
    title = "How Many Streets by gender names?",
    x = "",
    y = ""
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Gender",
    values = gender_colors,
    breaks = c("FEMALE", "MALE", "NEUTRAL"),
    labels = c("Women", "Men", "Not attributed"),
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  black_bg_theme_barplot()

```

## Streets Proportion by Gendered Names

```{r plot_proportion_by_gender}

ggplot(
  gender_distribution,
  aes(x = pct * 100, y = reorder(city, n), fill = gender_name)
) +
  geom_col(position = position_stack(reverse = TRUE)) +
  labs(
    title = "How Many Streets by gender names?",
    x = "",
    y = ""
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Gender",
    values = gender_colors,
    breaks = c("FEMALE", "MALE", "NEUTRAL"),
    labels = c("Women", "Men", "Not attributed"),
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = subset(gender_distribution, gender_name == "FEMALE"),
    aes(x = pct*100+5, label = paste(round(pct*100), "%", sep="")),
    hjust = 0,
    color = "white",
    size = 8
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  black_bg_theme_barplot()

```

```{r prepare_data}
df.street.points$gender_name[is.na(df.street.points$gender_name)] <- "NOT_HUMAN"
df.street.points$gender_name <- factor(df.street.points$gender_name, levels <- gender_names)
df.street.points <- df.street.points %>%
  arrange(city) %>%
  arrange(desc(gender_name))

```

# Mapping

```{r plot_city_function}

plot_city_streets <- function(city_name) {
  df.city.street.points = subset(df.street.points, city == city_name)

  ggplot(
    df.city.street.points,
    aes(
      lon,
      lat,
      group = id_segment,
      colour = gender_name
    )
  ) +
    geom_path(
      aes(linewidth = ifelse(gender_name %in% c("MALE", "FEMALE"), 1, 0.5))
    ) +
    scale_linewidth_identity() +
    scale_color_manual(
      name = "Gender",
      values = gender_colors,
      breaks = c("FEMALE", "MALE", "NOT_HUMAN"),
      labels = c("Women", "Men", "Others"),
      drop = FALSE
    ) +
    guides(
      colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 3))
    ) +
    coord_quickmap() +
    theme_void(base_size = 22) +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      legend.key = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      plot.title = element_text(color = "white"),
      legend.title = element_text(color = "white"),
      legend.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    )
}
```

```{r plot_all_cities, results='asis'}
cities <- unique(df.street.points$city)

for (city in cities) {
  cat("\n\n##", city, "\n\n")
  print(plot_city_streets(city))
  cat("\n\n")
}
```
